summary: Replaced raw FileNotFoundError + CalledProcessError tracebacks in the reusable supervisor with a single ConfigError exception class plus targeted catch-and-rephrase in policy.load_json, policy.load_queue, and policy.git_dirty_paths. CLI entry points run_next.py and build_context.py now catch ConfigError at the entry function and print a two-line 'stop: <reason>' + 'hint: <fix>' message on stderr with exit code 2 instead of a Python traceback. The consumer-adoption smoke assertions were updated to lock in the new shape (assert 'queue file not found' + path + example-slices.json hint for missing queue; assert 'not a Git repository' + 'git init -b main' hint for non-Git directory; assert no 'Traceback' substring in any consumer-facing failure). Docs updated to describe the friendly shape with the exact text a consumer sees. No source schema or contract changes.
validations_passed: manual probe: bootstrap temp consumer, supervisor + context with no queue + non-Git dir both print friendly two-line stop+hint messages

consumer_failure_modes_improved.before: FileNotFoundError traceback exposing python pathlib internals.
consumer_failure_modes_improved.after: stop: queue file not found: <abs path>\nhint: copy automation/examples/example-slices.json to that path and edit it for this repository's slices.

consumer_failure_modes_improved.condition: Consumer directory is not a Git repository
consumer_failure_modes_improved.after: stop: not a Git repository: <abs path>\nhint: run 'git init -b main' in this directory, commit the bootstrap, then re-run.

consumer_failure_modes_improved.before: FileNotFoundError from subprocess.run with the system errno noise.
consumer_failure_modes_improved.after: stop: git executable not found on PATH: <err>\nhint: install Git, or ensure git is on PATH for this process.
consumer_failure_modes_improved.asserted_by: Not directly asserted in this slice (would require a test that scrubs PATH). The code path exists and uses the same ConfigError mechanism as the other two; a follow-up could add the assertion.
Not directly asserted in this slice. The code path uses the same ConfigError mechanism; a follow-up could add a test that writes a malformed slices.json and runs the supervisor.

gaps_named_for_follow_up: git-not-on-PATH and malformed-JSON failure paths use the same ConfigError mechanism but are not asserted by tests; a follow-up smoke-test slice could lock those in too.

policy.load_handoff and other load_json call sites now also raise ConfigError on missing/invalid input. run_next.py catches ValueError for invalid handoff payloads (the existing code path) but only catches ConfigError at the top-level entry function; missing handoff files will print the friendly stop+hint and exit 2, which is the correct behavior but should be confirmed by a future smoke test.
Consumer adoption beyond the smoke level (real third-party migration, prompt overrides, Makefile/hooks/CI portability) remains unimplemented.
not_claimed: All possible failure paths in the reusable supervisor now emit friendly stop+hint messages. Only the three highest-visibility paths (missing queue, missing git repo, plus the JSON load wrappers feeding them) were touched. A schema validation failure (ValueError in load_queue) still surfaces with the existing 'Invalid queue file:' prefix, which was already friendly.
A real third-party repository has consumed the reusable package end-to-end.
The Makefile, hooks, prompts, or CI integration have been proven portable to a real consumer.
The two not-asserted code paths (git-not-on-PATH, malformed JSON) have been smoke-tested; they were implemented but the proof for those is limited to manual probing.
residual_risks: If a future contributor adds a new policy.load_* call site that raises FileNotFoundError directly without going through load_json, the friendly-message contract will not apply there. The ConfigError mechanism is the contract; new code paths need to opt into it explicitly.

The exact stop+hint message text is asserted as a substring match in the smoke tests. Editing the hint text in policy.py will require updating the matching test assertions. The asserted substrings ('queue file not found', 'example-slices.json', 'not a Git repository', "git init -b main") are deliberately stable phrasings.

If the consumer's REPO_ROOT resolution ever changes (e.g., a future supervisor edit that reads cwd instead of the script-relative path), the asserted absolute path in the failure messages would also change shape.
recommended_next_reason: Queue is empty after this slice. Natural follow-up boundaries (not queued): a real third-party repo migration when a target is named, prompt-fragment override portability proof, consumer Makefile / hooks / CI smoke, or smoke coverage for the two not-asserted ConfigError paths.
